resources.string: Browse all %1$d components built with Compose Foundation. No Material3. Copy, paste, own.
A container with border, shadow, and structured sections.
Page layout with top bar, bottom bar, and FAB slots.
Custom scrollbar container for scrollable content.
Toggle content visibility with a trigger.
Interactive button with 6 variants, 4 sizes, and 3 animations.
Single-line text input with focus animations.
Multi-line text input with char count.
Dropdown select with search and animations.
Draggable range input with customizable track.
Small status indicator with 4 variants and 3 sizes.
Data table with hover, stripe, and border styles.
Styled lists with bullets, numbers, or custom content.
Modal dialog with header and footer.
Action menu with items and labels.
Bottom navigation with animated pill indicator.
Header bar with title and actions.
A shadcn/ui-inspired component library and design system for Compose Multiplatform.
RikkaUI provides beautifully styled, copy-paste components for Jetpack Compose and Compose Multiplatform. Built entirely on compose.foundation with zero Material3 dependency.
The name means snowflake (\u516D\u82B1) or composing elements into harmony (\u7ACB\u82B1).
Every component is built on compose.foundation only. You get full control over styling without fighting Material3 defaults.
Components are designed to be copied into your project. No dependency lock-in \u2014 you own the code.
Every interactive component has configurable animation enums (Spring, Tween, None). This is something shadcn/ui can\u2019t do.
Works on Android, iOS, Desktop (JVM), and Web (Wasm). Same components, same API, everywhere.
20 semantic color tokens, 5 palettes, 7 accent colors, 5 style presets. Every token is overridable.
RikkaUI works in both Compose Multiplatform (KMP) projects and native Android projects that use Jetpack Compose. No KMP setup required for Android-only projects \u2014 just add the dependency and go.
Add RikkaUI to your Compose Multiplatform or native Android project.
RikkaUI is split into two modules. You only need to depend on :components \u2014 it transitively includes :foundation.
Colors, typography, spacing, shapes, motion tokens. RikkaTheme provider.
41 styled components built on compose.foundation. Depends on :foundation.
For KMP projects targeting Android, iOS, Desktop, and/or Web:
The KMP Gradle plugin automatically resolves the correct platform artifact for each target (Android, iOS, JVM, WasmJs).
RikkaUI works in standard Android projects that use Jetpack Compose. No KMP setup required \u2014 Gradle resolves the Android artifact automatically.
The API is identical across all platforms. Components, theme tokens, and animations work the same way whether you\u2019re targeting Android only or going multiplatform.
Like shadcn/ui, you can also copy component source files directly into your project. Each component is self-contained and depends only on compose.foundation and the RikkaUI theme tokens.
Copy the theme files from :foundation into your project.
Copy the component files you need from :components.
Update package names to match your project structure.
Customize colors, typography, and spacing to your brand.
RikkaUI has zero Material3 dependency. Uses compose.foundation only.
After adding the dependency, verify it works with a minimal example:
Customize every aspect of RikkaUI with palettes, accents, style presets, and individual token overrides.
Wrap your app in RikkaTheme. One function call gives you colors, typography, spacing, shapes, and motion tokens for all components.
5 base palettes, each with light and dark variants. Each palette defines 20 semantic color tokens in background/foreground pairs.
Pure white bg (light), neutral dark bg. Clean, minimal.
7 accent presets override the primary, primaryForeground, and ring tokens. Layer on top of any palette to change the brand color without touching everything else.
Uses the palette\u2019s built-in primary color. No override.
Style presets bundle shapes, spacing, motion, and type scale into a single choice. Pass a preset to RikkaTheme to change the entire visual feel.
4dp radius, 3dp spacing, snappy motion, scale 0.9. Sharp, dense.
20dp radius, 5dp spacing, playful motion, scale 1.05. Rounded, bouncy.
14dp radius, 5dp spacing, default motion, scale 1.1. Spacious, large.
0dp radius, 3dp spacing, minimal motion, scale 0.85. Square, tight.
For full control, override individual token factories. The palette + accent overload still works alongside manual overrides.
Every palette provides 20 semantic color tokens. Components use these tokens instead of raw colors, so switching palettes automatically updates your entire UI.
The main page/screen background. Used for the root surface of your app.
Default text color on the background. Used for body text, headings, and icons.
Background for Card, Dialog, and Sheet surfaces. Slightly elevated from background.
Text color inside cards, dialogs, and sheets.
Background for floating surfaces like Popover, Tooltip, Select dropdown, and DropdownMenu.
Your brand/accent color. Used for default Button, Toggle checked state, Progress bar, and focused inputs.
Text/icon color on primary surfaces. Must contrast with primary (e.g., white on blue).
Background for less prominent actions. Used by Button Secondary variant and Badge Secondary.
Danger/error color. Used by Button Destructive, Alert Destructive, and Badge Destructive.
Subdued background for disabled states, Skeleton placeholders, and inactive tab backgrounds.
Low-emphasis text color. Used for placeholder text, descriptions, and labels.
Subtle highlight for hover states. Used by Ghost buttons on hover and dropdown item highlights.
Default border for Card, Table, Separator, and dividers.
Border color for Input, Textarea, Select, and other form controls in their resting state.
Focus ring color shown around interactive elements when focused. Accent overrides change this token too.
Triggers an action or event. Supports 6 visual variants, 4 sizes, and 3 press animation styles.
Called when the button is clicked.
Visual variant: Default, Outline, Secondary, Ghost, Destructive, Link.
Shows a Spinner and disables interaction.
Button content. Receives the resolved foreground Color for children.
A container that groups related content with visual separation.
Your project is using the default theme with Zinc palette and Blue accent.
Click handler. When non-null the card is clickable.
Custom shadow elevation. Overrides variant default.
Vertically stacked sections that expand and collapse. Use it for FAQs, settings panels, or any grouped content.
Control which alerts you receive. Enable push notifications for new messages, mentions, and activity updates.
Manage who can see your profile and activity. Choose between public, friends-only, or private visibility.
Customize your theme, font size, and color accent. Changes apply across all devices.
Toggle this section to see the %1$s animation in action. Try switching between styles to compare.
Each AccordionItem manages its own state independently, so multiple sections can stay open at the same time.
Free standard shipping on orders over $50. Express delivery available at checkout.
30-day return policy for unused items. Contact support to start a return request.
Header text in the clickable title row.
Whether the content section is visible.
Called when the user toggles the section.
Icon for the expand indicator. Rotates 90 degrees when expanded.
A status container for displaying important messages with optional icon and entrance animation.
Your session has expired. Please log in again.
You can add components using the CLI.
A new version has been released.
A modal dialog for destructive-action confirmations. The scrim does not dismiss -- users must choose an action.
This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete your account and remove your data.
This will configure your workspace with the recommended settings.
Whether the alert dialog is visible.
Called when cancel action is triggered.
Called when confirm action is triggered.
Modifier applied to the dialog card.
Maximum width of the dialog card.
A circular container displaying user initials with optional status indicator and entrance animation.
1-2 character initials shown as avatar content.
Status dot: Online, Offline, Busy, or null.
A small status indicator or label for tags, counts, and notifications.
The label text displayed in the badge.
Visual variant: Default, Secondary, Destructive, Outline.
Displays the user\'s current location in a page hierarchy with clickable navigation links.
List of breadcrumb items to display.
Max items before collapsing middle items into an ellipsis. 0 = show all.
Click handler for the ellipsis element.
Display text for the breadcrumb item.
A boolean toggle rendered as a square box with an animated checkmark. Supports spring, tween, and instant animations.
Called when the checkbox state changes.
Label text rendered beside the checkbox and used for accessibility.
A primitive expand/collapse container. Unlike Accordion, it has no built-in header \u2014 you compose the trigger and content yourself.
This collapsible uses the DSL builder with internally-managed state.
Called when the trigger is clicked.
A long-press triggered overlay menu. On desktop/web maps to right-click, on mobile to long-press.
Builder for items, labels, and separators.
Modifier applied to the trigger wrapper.
Minimum width of the context menu.
Maximum width of the context menu.
Trigger area that responds to long-press.
A modal overlay that displays content in a centered card with a dismissible scrim.
Make changes to your profile here.
Your name and avatar will be visible to others.
This is a simple dialog with just text content.
Called when the user dismisses the dialog.
Modifier applied to the dialog card.
Maximum width of the dialog card.
A click-triggered overlay menu with scrollable actions, labels, and separators.
Whether the dropdown popup is visible.
Called when clicking outside to dismiss.
Modifier applied to the trigger wrapper.
Minimum width of the dropdown panel.
Maximum width of the dropdown panel.
The composable that anchors the menu.
A floating card that appears when hovering over a trigger element. Stays visible while the cursor is on the trigger or card.
A shadcn-inspired component library for Compose Multiplatform.
Modifier applied to the outer wrapper.
Delay in ms before showing the card.
Delay in ms before hiding the card.
Maximum width of the popup card.
The composable that triggers on hover.
Content displayed inside the floating card.
A single-line text field with animated focus states, icon slots, and optional character count.
Icon displayed before the text field.
Icon displayed after the text field.
Shows a clear (X) button when text is non-empty.
Displays character count when maxLength is set.
A styled inline indicator for keyboard shortcuts and key combinations.
List of individual key labels for KbdCombo.
Size variant applied to each key badge.
A text label for form elements with optional required indicator and disabled state.
When true, the label is dimmed with mutedForeground color.
When true, a red asterisk (*) is appended.
Styled lists with bullet points, numbers, or custom content per item.
Add the dependency to your project
Use any component from the library
Composable DSL for complex list items
List of string items to render.
A bottom navigation bar for app-level navigation with icon + label items and an animated indicator pill.
Whether this item is currently active.
Called when the item is tapped.
Text label displayed below the icon.
When false, label fades when not selected.
Override for the pill indicator color.
A smart page navigation component with automatic ellipsis and Previous/Next buttons.
Called when a different page is selected.
Max page buttons visible at once.
Active page animation: Scale, Fade, None.
Custom content for the previous button.
Custom content for the next button.
A click-triggered floating card anchored to a trigger element. Dismisses on outside click.
Whether the popover popup is visible.
Called when clicking outside to dismiss.
Modifier applied to the trigger wrapper.
Maximum width of the popup card.
The composable that anchors the popover.
Content displayed inside the floating card.
An animated horizontal progress bar that fills from left to right to indicate completion.
Background track color. Defaults to theme muted.
Fill bar color. Defaults to theme primary.
A selection control rendered as a circle with an animated inner dot. Use in groups for single-choice selection.
Whether the radio button is selected.
Called when the radio button is clicked.
Whether the radio button is interactive.
Label text rendered beside the circle and used for accessibility.
Page-level slot-based layout structure with top bar, bottom bar, FAB, and snackbar host.
Content area receives PaddingValues that account for bar heights.
The FAB is positioned at the bottom-end with theme-based padding.
Content respects custom insets (16dp left/right, 24dp bottom).
Modifier applied to the outer container.
Slot rendered at the top, spanning full width.
Slot rendered at the bottom, spanning full width.
FAB slot at bottom-end above the bottom bar.
Snackbar/toast host above bottom bar and FAB.
Main content. Receives PaddingValues for bars + insets.
Scrollable container with a custom scrollbar indicator.
Thickness of the scrollbar track and thumb.
Optional thumb color override. Defaults to mutedForeground.
A dropdown select component with animated popup, hover highlighting, and selection indicators.
Called with the new value on selection.
Available options with value and label.
Text shown when no option is selected.
A thin line that visually divides content sections.
Override color. Defaults to theme border color.
Line style: Solid, Dashed, or Dotted.
A panel that slides in from any edge of the screen with a dismissible scrim overlay.
Called when the user dismisses the sheet.
Edge to slide from: Right, Left, Top, Bottom.
Modifier applied to the sheet panel.
Enter/exit animation: Slide, FadeScale, Fade, None.
An animated loading placeholder that indicates content is being loaded.
Modifier for layout. Set size via Modifier.size/height.
Clip shape. Use shapes.full for circular.
A draggable range input for selecting a value between 0 and 1. Supports tap-to-seek and configurable animations.
Called when the user drags or taps to change the value.
Background track color. Defaults to theme muted.
Filled track color. Defaults to theme primary.
Thumb fill color. Defaults to theme background.
A circular loading indicator with configurable animation, size, and color.
Arc color. Defaults to theme primary.
Background track ring color. Null for no track.
A structured data table with hover highlights, striped rows, and configurable border styles.
Row visual effect: Hover, Stripe, None.
A set of layered sections of content that display one panel at a time.
Whether this tab is currently active.
Called when the tab is clicked.
Label text displayed in the tab.
A typography component that renders text with theme-aware styles and heading semantics.
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
This text can be selected and copied.
Typography variant: H1, H2, H3, H4, P, Lead, Large, Small, Muted.
Override color. Uses variant default when unspecified.
Maximum number of lines to display.
Minimum number of lines to occupy.
When true, wraps text in a SelectionContainer.
Override style merged on top of variant style.
A multi-line text input with animated focus states and optional character count.
Displays character count when maxLength is set.
A non-intrusive notification system with auto-dismiss, swipe gestures, and variant accents.
State holder managing the toast queue.
Modifier applied to the host container.
Anchor position: TopCenter, TopRight, BottomCenter, BottomRight.
Enter/exit animation: SlideIn, Fade, Scale, None.
Show countdown progress bar on each toast.
A boolean on/off switch with spring-animated thumb and smooth color transitions. Two sizes available.
Called when the toggle state changes.
A horizontal group of toggle buttons for mutually exclusive selection.
Label text displayed in the toggle item.
Whether this item is currently active.
Called when the item is clicked.
A small popup label that appears on hover with inverted theme colors for contrast.
Text displayed inside the tooltip popup.
Modifier applied to the outer wrapper.
Position relative to content: Top, Bottom, Start, End.
Delay in ms before showing the tooltip.
The composable that triggers the tooltip on hover.
A header bar with title, navigation icon, and action slots. Supports solid and transparent variants.
When true, title is centered horizontally.
Add components to your project from the command line. The RikkaUI CLI fetches components from the registry, rewrites package imports, and resolves dependencies automatically.
Download and install the CLI with a single command. Requires Java 17+.
Or download the JAR manually from GitHub Releases and run it with java -jar rikkaui.jar.
Initialize a configuration file in your project. The CLI detects whether you have a KMP or Android project and suggests defaults.
Creates a rikka.json file with your package name, components directory, and registry URL.
Add one or more components to your project. Dependencies are resolved automatically.
When you add a component that depends on others (e.g. Button depends on Icon, Spinner, Text), all dependencies are fetched and installed automatically.
List all available components in the registry, grouped by category with dependency info.
The rikka.json file stores your project defaults. You can create it with rikkaui init or let the CLI prompt you on first use.
Fetches component source from the registry at rikkaui.dev/r/{name}.json
Resolves all transitive dependencies (e.g. Button pulls Icon, Spinner, Text)
Rewrites package and import lines from the library namespace to your target package
Writes .kt files into your components directory, organized by subfolder
Foundation imports (theme tokens, colors, typography) are left untouched. You still need dev.rikkaui:foundation as a Gradle dependency.
Add foundation to your project, then paste this file anywhere in your source.
Source code not available for this component.
